summary: The Goshen Township Board of Zoning Appeals held a public hearing on February 28, 2022. The board approved a resolution for the Cincinnati United Soccer Club conditional use request previously granted, continued a sidewalk variance case for CDDG Goshen LLC pending more information from ODOT, and denied a front-yard privacy fence variance for the same applicant at 1910 State Route 28.
decisions: Approved minutes from the last meeting
Approved Resolution R-012-2022 granting conditional use for Cincinnati United Soccer Club for an active park with indoor training facility and five athletic fields
Continued Case 2022-001 (sidewalk variance for CDDG Goshen LLC) to the next meeting pending ODOT clarification on liability
Denied the front-yard privacy fence variance for CDDG Goshen LLC at 1910 State Route 28
action_items: Staff to contact ODOT to get a defined answer regarding sidewalk liability and whether the property owner can sign the maintenance agreement directly
Staff to present ODOT findings to the board at the next meeting (March 22, 2022)
Staff to print evaluation criteria handouts for board members at the next meeting
key_amounts: 39,600 square foot indoor training facility for Cincinnati United Soccer Club
30 foot maximum height netting approved for soccer facility
250 feet minimum activity area distance from residential property
100 feet minimum structure setback from all lot lines
50% open requirement for front yard fences (privacy fence is 100% closed)
notable_topics: Resolution R-012-2022 for Cincinnati United Soccer Club conditional use approval
Sidewalk variance request for CDDG Goshen LLC in TC district along State Route 28
ODOT sidewalk maintenance liability issue - township trustees declined responsibility
Front-yard privacy fence variance for CDDG Goshen LLC at 1910 State Route 28
TC district connectivity and sidewalk requirements for new development
Setting precedent for future sidewalk variance cases
notes: This is a Board of Zoning Appeals (BZA) meeting, not a Board of Trustees meeting. The sidewalk variance case was particularly complex due to ODOT requiring either the township or property owner to accept liability for sidewalk maintenance in state right-of-way. The board was concerned about setting precedent for future sidewalk variance requests. The privacy fence had been installed without proper approval and extended past the front of the building into the front yard. Next meeting scheduled for March 22, 2022 at 6:30 PM. Meeting adjourned at 7:17 PM.
